overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination fee (2× royalty) may not be collectible in CA

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee may be free to compete immediately after termination/expiration in CA

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration in Minneapolis may be challenged under CA B&P Code §20040.5
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee may be able to arbitrate/litigate in CA

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code §31512) or Franchise Relations Act (B&P Code §§20000-20043)
overrides.why_it_matters: Renewal/transfer release is narrower in CA — state franchise law claims survive

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Fraud-in-the-inducement claims preserved regardless of what franchisee signed

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance
overrides.why_it_matters: Same as CA — fraud-in-the-inducement claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law (815 ILCS 705/19 and 705/20) governs the franchise agreement for IL franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law on termination and renewal supersedes contract terms

overrides.override_summary: Forum selection outside IL is void (but arbitration outside IL is permitted)
overrides.why_it_matters: Litigation must stay in IL; arbitration can still be in Minneapolis

overrides.override_summary: Release or acknowledgment cannot negate claims under IL Franchise Disclosure Act
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law claims survive any release signed at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: All initial fees deferred until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations and outlet opens (MD Securities Commissioner financial assurance requirement)
overrides.why_it_matters: No upfront fee risk — full fee due only after restaurant is open. Signals regulatory concern about ADQ's financial condition.

overrides.override_summary: Claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law may be brought in Maryland
overrides.why_it_matters: No need to go to Minneapolis for MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: General release excludes liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law claims survive renewal/transfer release

overrides.override_summary: Good cause required for termination with 30 days to cure. Fair compensation on non-renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Stronger termination protections than base agreement

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation outside MI not enforceable (but franchisee can agree to out-of-state arbitration at time of arbitration)
overrides.why_it_matters: Arbitration must start in MI; can agree to move later

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot refuse transfer without good cause
overrides.why_it_matters: Lower bar for transfer approval in MI

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days notice for non-renewal required by MN Stat §80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Longer notice periods than base agreement (which has 10-30 day cure periods)

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside MN, waiver of jury trial, consent to injunctive relief, or consent to liquidated damages/termination penalties
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law provides maximum protection for venue/procedure

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot require general release that would waive MN franchise law rights (MN Rule 2860.4400D)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims survive any release

overrides.override_summary: ADQ will undertake defense of any third-party trademark infringement claim involving DQ/DQ Grill & Chill trademarks
overrides.why_it_matters: Unlike base agreement (no obligation to defend), MN addendum requires ADQ defense at ADQ's cost

overrides.override_summary: Termination fee provision DELETED ENTIRELY. ND Securities Commissioner determined liquidated damages/termination penalties are unfair.
overrides.why_it_matters: No termination fee in ND — significant financial benefit vs. base agreement

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete enforceable only under certain conditions per ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: Noncompete may be unenforceable in ND

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration must be at city nearest to authorized location with AAA office, not Minneapolis
overrides.why_it_matters: No out-of-state arbitration for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Waiver of exemplary and punitive damages may not be enforceable; jury trial waiver may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain right to seek punitive damages and jury trial

overrides.override_summary: General release at renewal is not a waiver of liability under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law claims survive renewal release

overrides.override_summary: Provision restricting jurisdiction/venue outside RI or requiring application of other state's laws is void under RI Franchise Investment Act §19-28.1-14
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can litigate RI Act claims in RI under RI law

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in conflicts of law
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law supersedes contract in areas of termination and renewal

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration must be in WA or mutually agreed place. Litigation in WA not precluded.
overrides.why_it_matters: No forced Minneapolis arbitration for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot include WA Act rights except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel after agreement in effect
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong release limitation — independent counsel required

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to franchisor's reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: The $6,000 transfer fee may be reduced if it exceeds ADQ's actual costs

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning <$100K/yr and independent contractors earning <$250K/yr (indexed to inflation per RCW 49.62)
overrides.why_it_matters: Most restaurant-level employees would be exempt from noncompete in WA

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting or hiring employees of other franchisees or the franchisor (RCW 49.62.060)
overrides.why_it_matters: Full labor mobility between DQ franchisees in WA
